title: Vima-Da-Boda

{{Quote|Vima betrayed her own. Vima tried to forget. In the time of dying, Vima hurled herself down among the lost... to escape the Great Scourge. Everyone was hunted down, and everyone was killed. But you! You live! You contain the spark that will rekindle the fire.|Vima to [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]|<ref name="DE 3">[[Dark Empire 3|''Dark Empire'' 3]]</ref>}}
'''Vima-Da-Boda''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] of the [[Sunrider family]] who survived the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] and the downfall of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]]. She spoke in the third person, referring to herself as "Vima."

Vima had an ancient [[Vima-Da-Boda's lightsaber|lightsaber]] that was many millennia old. It was built in [[10,000 BBY]]. It eventually fell into the hands of a [[Unidentified female Jedi Master|Female Jedi Master]]. This Master gave it to [[Vima-Da-Boda's Master|her Padawan]], who gave it to Vima-Da-Boda, who in turn gave it to Leia, years later, on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]. Leia used the lightsaber to slice the hand off of an [[Imperial Sentinel]], before [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] caused it to explode.

==Biography==
===Jedi Knight===
Vima Da-Boda was a descendant of [[Vima Sunrider]], daughter of legendary [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] of [[Old Sith Wars|ancient times]] [[Nomi Sunrider]],<ref name="EGC" /> and served [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] for over a century. She was trained by a [[Vima-Da-Boda's Master|male Jedi Master]] who gave her a 10,000-year-old [[Vima-Da-Boda's lightsaber|lightsaber]]. The [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]] passed through Vima to her daughter, [[Neema-Da-Boda|Neema]], and Vima began to train her daughter in the ways of the Jedi. As with many young pupils, Neema grew frustrated by the slow pace of her Jedi education, and sought alternative training. Neema's impatience and lack of knowledge in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] opened her to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. The dark side consumed Neema, and she left her mother.<ref name="Endnotes" />

Years later, Vima received a telepathic plea from her daughter, who had become the wife of an [[Ottethan warlord]]. Vima rushed to the dungeon to her daughter's aid, but was too late: Neema had been fed to the [[Rancor/Legends|rancors]] of Ottethan. Giving in to her rage, Vima sliced the warlord in half with her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Her desire for vengeance quenched, Vima realized she was succumbing to the dark side herself. Over time, her ability to sense and use the Force slowly faded away, although it is unclear if this was a result of her fall to the dark side or of another cause.<ref name="Endnotes" />

===Hiding===
With the onset of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Vima went into hiding on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]. Her weak connection to the Force coupled with Nar Shaddaa's dense population may have been the key to her survival of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] and the subsequent Jedi eradication efforts.<ref name="Endnotes" />

Shortly after the start of the Great Jedi Purge, Vima was found by Jedi Master [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], who had come to Nar Shaddaa with the infant [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] while seeking passage to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Vima recognized Kenobi as a fellow Jedi and told him her story. After informing him of the presence of a man and woman on the Smugglers' Moon whom Kenobi believed to be Masters [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]] and [[T'ra Saa/Legends|T'ra Saa]], Vima offered him her lightsaber but Kenobi turned it down.<ref name="Lone Wolf">{{StoryCite|story=Lone Wolf: A Tale of Obi-Wan and Luke|book=SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story}}</ref>

In [[5 BBY/Legends|5 BBY]] Vima ran across [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] for the first time and spoke of his future, telling him of [[Mako Spince]]'s betrayal, his joining a cause and becoming a General and his willingness to give up the smuggler's life for [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|the woman he loved]]. He brushed her off then as being insane and forgot her by the next morning.<ref name="THG">''[[The Hutt Gambit]]''</ref>

At some point between [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] and [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], Vima was imprisoned on [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] for an unknown crime or reason. Vima discovered [[Kyp Durron]], a young Human ripe with nascent Force potential. Even with her dulled connection to the Force, Vima could sense the raw, untapped power within Durron. Using what connection she had, Vima taught Durron what minor skills she could before being removed by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] authorities under unknown circumstances.<ref name="Jedi Search" />

===Re-emergence===
[[File:Vima-Da-Boda.jpg|thumb|left|185px|Vima-Da-Boda]]
By [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], Vima had made her way back to Nar Shaddaa. In that year, she sensed the Force presence of [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]. Detecting what she thought to be a Jedi, Vima explained the tale of her fall and bequeathed to Organa Solo a protective box<ref name="DE 3" /> containing her lightsaber.<ref name="Endnotes" />

After the apparent death of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] with the [[Battle of Pinnacle Base|destruction]] of the [[Eclipse (Eclipse-class)|Eclipse]],<ref name="DE 6">[[Dark Empire 6|''Dark Empire'' 6]]</ref> Organa Solo returned to Nar Shaddaa to get Vima to join her and share her knowledge of the destroyed Jedi Order. Vima, having realized her errors decades earlier, felt unworthy of serving the power she sensed in Organa Solo and the unborn [[Anakin Solo]], but was given little choice after an ambush by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]. [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] helped the group escape the clutches of Fett.<ref name="DEII 2">[[Dark Empire II 2|''Dark Empire II'' 2]]</ref>

En route to [[New Alderaan/Legends|New Alderaan]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] was forced to hide from Fett in the gas cloud home to [[Empatojayos Brand]]'s ''[[Robida Colossus]]''.<ref name="DEII 4">[[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]]</ref> Upon their arrival, Vima and Brand aided Skywalker in the training of [[Jem Ysanna]], [[Rayf Ysanna]], and [[Kam Solusar]], the first of Skywalker's Jedi students.<ref name="DEII 5">[[Dark Empire II 5|''Dark Empire II'' 5]]</ref> Shortly thereafter, Vima used her healing skills to rid Skywalker of a poison injected into him by [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] agents.<ref name="DE2_6_HoD">[[Dark Empire II 6|''Dark Empire II'' 6]]</ref>

After fleeing to [[Nespis VIII]], Leia, who had been hesitant to tap into her Force potential, learned the ways of the Jedi from Vima, including how to shield the newly born Anakin. This skill allowed Organa Solo to save baby Anakin from being possessed by the spirit of Palpatine. Vima, having rekindled her [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] spirit in aiding Organa Solo, Skywalker and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], claimed to be able to feel the flow of the Force once more.<ref name="Empire's End" />

[[File:VimaLuke-CEIII1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Vima warns Skywalker about a threat to the New Republic]]
Little is known of the fate of Vima after the defeat of Palpatine on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]].<ref name="HB3" /> For a period of time, she helped instruct new Jedi recruits at Luke Skywalker's [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]],<ref name="A Guide to the Star Wars Universe (Third Edition)">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> and in [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]] she warned Skywalker about [[Ennix Devian]]. By that time, she had ceased referring to herself in the third person.<ref name="Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost 1">[[Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost 1|''Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost'' 1]]</ref> But after that, she seemed to have disappeared.<ref name="HB3" />

==Behind the scenes==
Vima-Da-Boda was voiced by [[Glynnis Talken Campbell|Glynnis Talken]] in the [[Dark Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Empire'']] and [[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio dramas]].

The epilogue included with [[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]] and the [[Star Wars: Empire's End (trade paperback)|''Empire's End'' trade paperback]] stated that Vima disappeared on Onderon in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]] during the New Republic celebration over the defeat of Palpatine, as did [[Star Wars Handbook 3: Dark Empire|''Star Wars Handbook'' 3: ''Dark Empire'']]. However, both ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' and the appendix of ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' said Vima served as an instructor at Luke Skywalker's Jedi Praxeum on Yavin 4 in her later years.
